title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Joseph Aoun, Northeastern University's president, earned over $3 million in FY 2025, making him the highest-paid college leader in Massachusetts.
Aoun's compensation increased sharply from FY 2021 to FY 2022, driven by deferred compensation and bonus payments.
Only Joseph Aoun and MIT's Sally Kornbluth reported over $2 million in compensation among college presidents in Massachusetts.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk St., Cambridge, around 5:30 a.m.
Investigators determined that Bautista was shot shortly before 4:30 a.m. in Cambridge, and found by a pedestrian an hour later.
Cambridge police and Massachusetts State Police are investigating the shooting; tips can be submitted anonymously to authorities.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major of the New England Aquarium volunteered in Cape Town, caring for endangered penguin chicks.
African penguins could become functionally extinct by 2035 without significant conservation efforts.
The New England Aquarium supports African penguin conservation through the SAFE program and annual trainer deployments.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras apologizes after a week of confrontations, including a brawl with the Nationals resulting in ejections and suspensions.
Contreras is appealing the seven-game suspension imposed by MLB and has attributed his recent emotions to personal stress factors.
Contreras's native Venezuela suffered major earthquakes, impacting his emotional state during games amid an ongoing standout season.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez from the Red Sox selected for the MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman achieved 33 strikeouts and 17 saves with a 2.10 ERA in 25.2 innings for 2026.
Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA, will appear in the All-Star Game held in his former city, Philadelphia.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but understanding over the Celtics trading Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Pierce encouraged Brown to use perceived disrespect as motivation, highlighting similar personal experiences from his own career.
Brown criticized Boston for a lack of respect and had concerns about Brad Stevens' handling of trade discussions.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated its Fourth of July festivities with a parade and a Declaration of Independence reading.
Thousands gathered for the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper.
A drone show and confetti cannons enhanced the spectacle at the Hatch Memorial Shell on the Charles River.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads Red Sox in OPS (.977) and has hit seven home runs in the last 30 days.
Durbin's performance turnaround follows working with a new personal hitting coach mid-season.
Durbin is under team control until 2031, offering the Red Sox salary cap flexibility.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman broke the all-time MLB strikeout record for relievers with his 1,364th strikeout against the Angels.
Chapman achieved the milestone in 847 innings pitched, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's record set over 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage; he expressed pride and fulfillment in his career milestone.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Allstate's 2026 report ranks Boston as the most collision-prone U.S. city, with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts rank near the bottom, with drivers experiencing collisions every 5.14 and 5.18 years, respectively.
Providence, Rhode Island ranks 194th with drivers averaging 5.87 years between crashes; Brownsville, Texas is the safest city.
